features.description: Latest available morning ASOS observation (temperature, dewpoint, wind, pressure tendency) up to the cutoff, used for same-day persistence / morning-state features.
features.publication_schedule: hourly, ~5-20 min after each valid hour
features.fallback_behavior: Step back one hour at a time to the last published observation; halt if no observation within max_staleness_hours of the cutoff.

features.description: Daily ASOS aggregates (TMAX/TMIN/dewpoint/wind) for the prior calendar day (D-1), the autoregressive and station-network backbone of every city model.
features.publication_schedule: hourly, ~5-20 min after each valid hour
features.fallback_behavior: Use the most recent fully-observed prior day; if the freshest ASOS observation is older than max_staleness_hours, halt (kill switch).

features.source: NWS MOS (GFS MAV / NAM MET) via IEM
features.description: Today's MOS maximum-temperature guidance and MOS-climatology anomaly -- the primary NWP-informed signal.  Uses the freshest MOS cycle provably published by 7am ET.
features.publication_schedule: 00Z run issued ~02-03Z; 06Z run issued ~08-09Z.  06Z guidance (~08-09Z = 03-04 ET) is out before the conservative 11:00 UTC cutoff; the 12Z run (issued ~14-15Z) is NOT.
features.fallback_behavior: Prefer the 06Z run; fall back to the 00Z run if 06Z is missing.  If neither cycle is available, drop MOS features and flag a kill-switch event (model is NWP-blind for the day).

features.description: Prior-day settled high-temperature outcome, used for realized-error tracking, calibration-drift monitoring, and persistence baselines.
features.publication_schedule: settles the morning after the contract day
features.fallback_behavior: Use the latest settled day; if the prior day has not settled by the cutoff, skip settlement-dependent monitoring features and warn (non-blocking) but flag for the calibration-drift kill switch.

features.description: Upper-air stability and 850mb thermal/wind features.  CRITICAL TIMING NOTE: the 12Z sounding (valid 12:00 UTC) is issued AFTER the 7am ET cutoff and must never feed live inference -- only the 00Z sounding of the market day is cutoff-safe.
features.publication_schedule: 00Z and 12Z launches; 00Z data available ~02-03Z.  12Z is post-cutoff.
features.fallback_behavior: Use the 00Z sounding of the market day; if absent, fall back to the prior day's 12Z sounding.  Soundings are recommended, not blocking: degrade gracefully and warn rather than halting.
